factors.summary: The company is in a restructuring mode, with layoffs and facility consolidations to reduce costs.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, with no strikes or significant disputes reported.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses for the three months ended June 30, 2022 decreased $3.6 million, or 0.8%, from the prior year comparable quarter, primarily due to lower variable compensation expense. factors.summary: Free cash flow has shown mixed performance, with some evidence indicating improvement and other evidence showing a deterioration. evidence.text: Free cash flow and free cash flow ratios provide both management and investors a measurement of cash generated from operations that is available to fund acquisitions, pay dividends, repay debt and repurchase our common stock.

Free cash flow for the six months ended June 30, 2022 decreased $262.1 million compared to the prior year period, due to lower operating cash flow primarily as a result of increases in inventory, compensation payouts, estimated tax payments, and investments in capital expenditures compared to the prior year.

factors.summary: The company's organic revenue growth is mixed. While there are segments experiencing solid demand and growth, particularly in North America retail fueling and vehicle wash solutions, other areas, such as EMV compliant equipment in North America, are facing reduced demand. Overall, the organic revenue growth for the second quarter of 2022 was modestly declining compared to the previous year.

evidence.text: The modest organic revenue decline was primarily driven by reduced year-over-year demand in North America for EMV compliant equipment following the compliance deadline in the second quarter of 2021. This was mostly offset by solid demand in our North America retail fueling, fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions business, along with pricing actions aimed at mitigating material and logistics cost inflation.

The 7.5% organic revenue growth for the second quarter of 2022 was broad-based across most of our businesses based on solid underlying demand and our ability to produce and ship despite supply chain constraints, input cost inflation, and unforecasted production interruptions.

evidence.text: The 7.5% organic revenue growth for the second quarter of 2022 was broad-based across most of our businesses based on solid underlying demand and our ability to produce and ship despite supply chain constraints, input cost inflation, and unforecasted production interruptions. The Engineered Products segment had organic revenue growth of 18.6% primarily as a result of pricing initiatives as well as strength in our waste handling, vehicle services, industrial automation, and industrial winch and hoist businesses, whereas our aerospace and defense business declined organically year-over-year driven by supply chain constraints and program timing.

evidence.text: Capacity utilization is increasing, driven by strong demand and production efforts.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2022 Compared to the Second Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Despite the strong organic growth and near record-high backlog levels for the segment, shipments continue to be challenged by supply chain and labor availability constraints.

evidence.text: We anticipate organic revenue growth to continue into the second half of the year as demand remains strong in several of our key end-markets, most notably waste handling and vehicle services.

factors.summary: The company identifies two key catalysts for growth: improvements in supply chain and logistics, and the successful integration of new product launches and recent acquisitions. These factors are expected to drive organic revenue growth in the near term, particularly in key end-markets such as retail fueling, fluid transfer, and vehicle wash businesses.

evidence.text: We anticipate organic revenue to increase in the second half of the year as demand remains constructive in our key end-markets, most notably the retail fueling, fluid transfer and vehicle wash businesses, and we anticipate improvements related to supply chain and logistics constraints.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2022 Compared to the Second Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Subsequent to the second quarter of 2022, on July 1, 2022, the Company completed the acquisition of Malema Engineering Corporation (''Malema''), a designer and manufacturer of flow measurement and control instruments serving customers in the biopharmaceutical, semiconductor and industrial sectors, for approximately $224.0 million, subject to customary post-closing adjustments, and contingent consideration of up to $50.0 million. The Malema acquisition expands the Company's biopharma single-use production offering within the Pumps & Process Solutions segment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvements in supply chain and successful product launches are expected to drive growth.

factors.summary: The company is managing its inventory levels effectively, with no significant risks of obsolescence or shortage. However, specific details about inventory levels are not provided in the second quarter comparison section.

evidence.text: No specific information on inventory levels is provided.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2022 Compared to the Second Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Bookings were $2.1 billion for the three months ended June 30, 2022, a decrease of $270.3 million, or 11.4% compared to the prior year comparable period. Included in this result was organic decline of 9.9%, an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 2.9%, disposition-related decline of 1.8%, and acquisition-related growth of 3.2%. The organic bookings decline was driven primarily by a $74.0 million order reversal due to customer financing limitations in our beverage can-making business, a decrease in orders for biopharmaceutical components used in COVID-19 vaccine production, and declines in our vehicle service and waste handling businesses principally related to higher year-over-year comparables and timing of orders in waste handling. This was partially offset by solid demand in our marking and coding business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is managing inventory levels effectively with no significant risks.

evidence.text: Throughout this Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations (''MD&A''), we refer to measures used by management to evaluate performance as well as liquidity, including a number of financial measures that are not defined under accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America (''GAAP''). We believe these measures provide investors with important information that is useful in understanding our business results and trends. Explanations within this MD&A provide more details on the use and derivation of these measures.

factors.summary: The company maintained stable R&D investment, with a slight decrease in expenses year-over-year. The evidence shows that R&D costs were $38.6 million in Q2 2022 and $41.2 million in Q2 2021, indicating a 6.3% decline. Over the six months, the expenses were $79.3 million and $82.4 million, respectively, showing a 3.7% decrease. The percentage of R&D costs relative to revenue also improved slightly, dropping from 2.0% to 1.8% for the three-month period and from 2.1% to 1.9% for the six-month period.

evidence.text: Research and development costs, including qualifying engineering costs, are expensed when incurred and amounted to $38.6 million and $41.2 million for the three months ended June 30, 2022 and 2021, respectively, and $79.3 million and $82.4 million, for the six months ended June 30, 2022 and 2021, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2022 Compared to the Second Quarter 2021

evidence.text: These costs as a percent of revenue were 1.8% and 2.0% for the three months ended June 30, 2022 and 2021, respectively, and 1.9% and 2.1% for the six months ended June 30, 2022 and 2021, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: R&D investment remained stable with slight decreases in expenses and improved percentage relative to revenue.

However, specific ESG commitments for the second quarter 2022 compared to 2021 were not explicitly mentioned.

evidence.text: Environmental and social considerations are part of the company's strategy, particularly in the Clean Energy & Fueling and Climate & Sustainability Technologies segments.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2022 Compared to the Second Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Our Clean Energy & Fueling segment provides components, equipment, and software and service solutions enabling safe transport of traditional and clean fuels and other hazardous substances along the supply chain, as well as the safe and efficient operation of convenience retail, retail fueling and vehicle wash establishments.

Our Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment is a provider of innovative and energy-efficient equipment and systems that serve the commercial refrigeration, heating and cooling and beverage can-making equipment markets.

factors.summary: End markets are growing, with notable strength in waste handling, vehicle services, and industrial automation.

evidence.text: This was mostly offset by solid demand in our North America retail fueling, fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions business, along with pricing actions aimed at mitigating material and logistics cost inflation. Strong demand in our core non-COVID-19 biopharma platform, industrial pumps, plastics and polymer processing solutions, and bearings and compression components businesses which all saw revenue growth driven by robust end market demand and strong backlogs.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2022 Compared to the Second Quarter 2021

evidence.text: The Engineered Products segment had organic revenue growth of 18.6% primarily as a result of pricing initiatives as well as strength in our waste handling, vehicle services, industrial automation, and industrial winch and hoist businesses.
